info.title: Imperial Trading API
info.description:
Imperial is a perpetual futures router on Solana — a single passthrough program that brokers
trades across multiple underlying perp venues (Jupiter, Flash Trade, Phoenix, GMTrade, …).
This HTTP API is the surface third-party bots and integrators use to authenticate, submit
orders, and read public on-chain state.

## Authentication

Trading endpoints are JWT-gated. The flow:

1. `POST /mobile/connect` — sign `imperial:mobile-connect:{wallet}:{nonce}` with your Solana
   keypair and post `{wallet, message, signature}`. Response: a one-time `code`.
2. `POST /mobile/exchange` — exchange the `code` for `{jwt, expires_at}`. The code expires in
   5 minutes and can only be redeemed once.
3. Send trading requests with `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`. The JWT's wallet claim must match
   the `wallet` field in every request body.
4. `POST /mobile/revoke` — revoke the JWT when you're done (e.g. bot shutdown, key rotation).

The JWT is a Solana-signature-anchored delegation: holding it is equivalent to letting the
operator place orders from the associated wallet's profiles. Treat it like an API key — copy
it once, store it securely, and revoke it if compromised.

Multi-wallet bots (e.g. fan-out traders): each wallet owner independently completes the flow
on their own dashboard and shares their JWT with the bot operator. The bot then holds N tokens
and calls the API in parallel, one request per wallet. Any wallet owner can revoke their token
anytime without affecting the others.

## Rate limits

Unified limit: **600 requests/minute sustained, burst of 120**. Keyed per-wallet when a valid
JWT is present, else per-IP. Exceeding the budget returns `429 Too Many Requests` with a
`Retry-After` header and `{ "error": "rate_limited", "retry_after_seconds": N }` body. The
`/health`, `/ws`, and Telegram webhook endpoints are not rate limited.

## WebSockets

Two endpoints. Both are unauthenticated and exempt from the rate limiter.

### `GET /ws` — wallet-scoped invalidation signals

Subscribe by wallet to learn when your own positions or orders change. Server messages are
coalesced refetch triggers; the API does not push full state here.

```json
{ "type": "subscribe", "wallet": "<base58-pubkey>" }
```

- `{ "type": "positions_updated" }` — fired on position open, close, DCA leg execution, and
  fill completion. Refetch `/positions`.
- `{ "type": "orders_updated" }` — fired on order creation, execution, and cancellation.
  Refetch `/orders` for the unified view, or `/passthrough/users/{wallet}/orders` for the
  passthrough subset only.

Ping/pong keepalive: send `{ "type": "ping" }`, receive `{ "type": "pong" }`. Reconnect with
backoff on drops — there is no replay buffer, so refetch on reconnect to resync.

### `GET /ws/market` — public market-data stream

Replaces polling on `/funding-rates`, `/mark-prices`, and `/phoenix/depth`. Each push carries
the full payload, so clients never have to refetch. On every successful `subscribe_*` the
server immediately pushes the current cached snapshot before tracking diffs — late
subscribers don't have to wait a publisher tick.

Subscribe / unsubscribe messages:

```json
{ "type": "subscribe_funding_rates" }
{ "type": "subscribe_mark_prices" }
{ "type": "subscribe_phoenix_depth" }                      // all symbols
{ "type": "subscribe_phoenix_depth", "symbols": ["SOL"] }  // filtered
{ "type": "unsubscribe_funding_rates" }
{ "type": "unsubscribe_mark_prices" }
{ "type": "unsubscribe_phoenix_depth" }
{ "type": "ping" }
```

Server-pushed events:

- `{ "type": "funding_rate_update", "symbol": "SOL", "venue": "phoenix" | "jupiter" |
  "flash" | "gmtrade", "source": <RateSource>, "longFundingRatePerHourPercent": <f64|null>,
  "shortFundingRatePerHourPercent": <f64|null>, "longBorrowRatePerHourPercent": <f64|null>,
  "shortBorrowRatePerHourPercent": <f64|null> }` — same shape as a per-venue entry on
  `/funding-rates`. The `source` discriminator carries provenance (live vs. fallback);
  filter on it the same way you would in the REST response.
- `{ "type": "mark_price_update", "symbol": "SOL", "venue": "phoenix" | "flash",
  "source": <PriceSource>, "price": <f64>, "fetchedAtUnixMs": <i64> }`.
- `{ "type": "phoenix_depth_update", "symbol": "SOL", "snapshot": <DepthSnapshot> }` —
  the `symbol` here is the **Phoenix-raw** symbol (`GOLD` rather than `XAU`), matching
  what `/phoenix/depth` returns.

Funding sign convention: `longFundingRatePerHourPercent > 0` means longs pay shorts.
Phoenix's short rate is the negation of its long rate (single-pool perp); GMTrade reports
both sides independently. Funding fields are `null` for venues that only expose borrow
rates (Jupiter, Flash) — populated borrow fields are non-negative regardless of side.

Symbols are canonical (`SOL`, `BTC`, `XAU`) on `funding_rate_update` and `mark_price_update`;
`phoenix_depth_update` uses Phoenix-raw symbols (`SOL`, `GOLD`, …). Translate accordingly.

## Order types

The `orderType` field on `/mobile/orders` takes a numeric code:

| Code | Name             | Notes                                                   |
|-----:|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
|    0 | Market           | `triggerPrice` ignored                                  |
|    1 | Limit            | Requires `triggerPrice`                                 |
|    2 | StopLimit        | Requires `triggerPrice`                                 |
|    3 | LandMine         | Requires `triggerPrice`, `extraData.waitPrice`, `waitDuration` |
|    4 | Ratchet          | Requires `extraData.worstPrice`, `ratchetSize`          |
|    6 | RatchetEntry     | Same extras as Ratchet                                  |
|    9 | Dca              | Requires `extraData.dcaStartPrice`, `dcaEndPrice`, `dcaNumLegs` |
|   10 | FibRatchet       | Same extras as Ratchet                                  |
|   11 | FibRatchetEntry  | Same extras as Ratchet                                  |
|   12 | DcaClose         | Requires `extraData.dcaCloseStartPrice`, `dcaCloseEndPrice`, `dcaCloseNumLegs` |
|   13 | DcaTimeClose     | Requires `extraData.dcaCloseIntervalSeconds`, `dcaCloseNumLegs` |
|   14 | DcaRatchetClose  | Requires `extraData.dcaCloseNumLegs`, `dcaCloseRatchetSize` |
|   15 | DcaTime          | Requires `extraData.dcaIntervalSeconds`, `dcaNumLegs`   |
|   16 | DcaRatchet       | Requires `extraData.dcaNumLegs`, `dcaRatchetSize`       |

`underwriter`: 0 = Jupiter, 1 = Flash Trade, 2 = Phoenix, 3 = GMTrade. (4 is reserved for
Pacifica and rejected today.) Wire format mirrors `passthrough_client::Underwriter`.
`side`: 0 = long, 1 = short.
`action`: 0 = Increase, 1 = Decrease.

## Subaccounts (profiles)

Every wallet has six implicit subaccounts indexed `0..5`. Each subaccount is its own
isolated-margin profile; balances and positions don't mix across them. There is no
"create subaccount" call — the on-chain profile PDA is created lazily by the order-bot
on the first `/deposit/build-tx` or first `/mobile/orders` placement that targets that
`profileIndex`. `/mobile/balances` will return `usdc: 0` for every uninitialized profile
without erroring; that's a normal "this index has never been used" response, not a
provisioning gate.

`profileIndex` is required on every order, deposit, and balance call. Pick a convention
(e.g. profile 0 for default, 1+ for strategy buckets) and stick with it — there's no
server-side notion of a "primary" profile.

## Trading bot quickstart

End-to-end flow for a bot that opens a position on one venue and hedges it on another.

1. **Auth** — `POST /mobile/connect` then `POST /mobile/exchange` to get a JWT (see
   "Authentication" above). All subsequent trading calls send `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`.
2. **Fund the profile** — `POST /deposit/build-tx` with `mode: "deposit"` returns a sponsored,
   partially-signed transaction that creates the profile (if new) and deposits USDC from the
   wallet's USDC ATA. Sign with the wallet's keypair and submit. Same endpoint with
   `mode: "withdraw"` pulls USDC back to the wallet. Skip if the profile is already funded
   for the size you want to trade.
3. **Pick a venue pair** — `GET /funding-rates` returns one row per canonical symbol with
   per-venue funding/borrow rates. Decide which venue to long and which to short. For a
   single-venue trade, `GET /route?asset=...&side=...&notional=...` returns a cost-optimized
   recommendation.
4. **Check available USDC** — `GET /mobile/balances` returns the per-subaccount USDC balance
   in 6-decimal native units. Size each leg against this; an underfunded profile fails the
   margin check on submission.
5. **Open the legs** — `POST /mobile/orders` with `action=0` (Increase). Use `symbol` (e.g.
   `"SOL"`, `"XAU"`) plus `underwriter` to address the market — the same canonical symbol
   you read from `/funding-rates`. The server resolves it to the right `marketMint` per
   venue, including Phoenix synthetics that have no SPL mint of their own. Existing
   integrations can still send `marketMint` directly. Fire the legs sequentially: if the
   second leg fails, immediately submit a Decrease for the first to unwind. The endpoint
   returns HTTP 200 even on rejection — always inspect `success` and `error` (see
   "Trading-endpoint response convention" below). Successful resting orders return
   `orderPda`, which you can pass straight to `/mobile/orders/cancel` or `/update` without
   waiting for indexer catch-up. Phoenix orders auto-activate the trader account on first
   use; you don't need to call `/phoenix/register` first. Per-venue market lists are still
   available at `/phoenix/markets`, `/flash/markets`, `/gmtrade/markets` if you need
   underlying details (orderbook PDAs, leverage tiers, fee tables).
6. **Track state** — subscribe to both WS endpoints. `/ws/market` pushes funding-rate / mark-
   price / depth updates so you don't have to poll. `/ws` (with `subscribe` keyed on your
   wallet) pings you when your positions or orders change; refetch `/positions` and `/orders`
   on each ping. There is no replay buffer on either socket — refetch on reconnect.
7. **Close the legs** — `POST /mobile/orders` with `action=1` (Decrease) on each leg. Use
   `sizeUsd` equal to the open size for a full close.
8. **Reclaim residue** — after closing a position whose collateral was non-USDC (long
   SOL/BTC/ETH custody on Jupiter or Flash leaves WSOL/WBTC/WETH on the profile), call
   `POST /passthrough/users/{wallet}/profiles/{index}/sync` to swap residue back to USDC
   and route to the user wallet. Idempotent. Pure-USDC venues (Phoenix shorts, USDC-margin
   profiles) don't need this step.

## Trading-endpoint response convention

`POST /mobile/orders[/batch|/cancel|/collateral|/update]` always returns HTTP 200 once the
request was well-formed and the order bot was reachable, even if the order was rejected
on-chain. Inspect the response body:

- `success: true` — order accepted, `signature` is the Solana tx signature.
- `success: false` — order rejected; `error` is a human-readable message and the raw bot
  error is logged server-side. Common cases: leverage too high, market closed, slippage
  exceeded, no matching open position for a close-side order.

HTTP 4xx is reserved for malformed input or auth failures (missing/invalid JWT, mismatched
wallet claim). HTTP 5xx is reserved for the server itself failing (database or order-bot
transport failures that aren't fully recoverable).

200.description: A partially-signed sponsored transaction. For `mode: "deposit"`, it creates the user-account PDA (if needed) and the profile's USDC ATA, then transfers USDC from the wallet's USDC ATA into the profile. For `mode: "withdraw"`, it creates the wallet's USDC ATA (if needed) and withdraws USDC from the profile back to the wallet. The operator signs for rent and ATA creation; the caller must sign with the wallet keypair before submission.

500.description: Order bot unreachable or build failed

200.description: Flash Trade markets, one row per (symbol, side). `maxLeverage`, `openPositionFeeRate`, and `volatilityFeeRate` are populated when Flash's API is reachable; falls back to a hardcoded list otherwise.

Unified funding & borrow rates keyed by canonical symbol. Each row has up to four per-venue entries (jupiter, flash, phoenix, gmtrade) — present only when that venue lists the symbol. The `source` field on each entry marks provenance (live vs. fallback). GMTrade exposes per-side funding directly; Phoenix funding comes from the venue's `market` WS channel (`phoenix_market_ws` source; rows still warming report `phoenix_clob_no_snapshot` with null rates); Jupiter and Flash use borrow rates.
Per-symbol GMTrade funding/borrow/net rates (percent per hour). Empty until the WS feed has populated.
Per-symbol open-interest headroom (USD) per side. Refreshed every 30s from the on-chain Market account.

GMTrade markets. A symbol may have multiple variants (per collateral pair); the `closed` flag reflects the on-chain `Market.flags.Closed` bit and is authoritative — the program rejects orders against a closed market with `MarketClosed (6127)`.

Unified mark prices keyed by canonical symbol, with one entry per venue listing the symbol. Phoenix mid (live orderbook WS), Flash CustomOracle (Pyth-pushed), Jupiter Doves AgPriceFeed (read directly via RPC every 1s), and GMTrade indexMin/indexMax mid (from its `indexTokens` WS, refreshed every 5min). The `source` field on each entry marks provenance; `fetchedAtUnixMs` lets you gate on freshness.

Per-subaccount USDC balances. Each entry is one Imperial profile (`profile_index` 0..5). USDC is held in the profile's USDC ATA in 6-decimal native units. Use this to size new orders before calling `/mobile/orders`.
One-time code to exchange for a JWT

400.description: Invalid message format or replayed nonce

200.description: JWT and expiry
Order outcome. `success=false` with an `error` message is a rejected-but-valid response.

400.description: Validation error (e.g. missing `extraData` for ratchet/DCA order types)

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked JWT, or JWT wallet doesn't match `wallet`

post.summary:
Submit an entry order and its attached close legs (TP/SL/ratchet/etc.)
in one request.
post.description:
The entry and close legs travel together so close-target validation is
implicit: a close leg whose tuple matches its entry is, by construction,
targeting the position that entry will create. This avoids the racy
"is the entry order indexed yet?" lookup the per-order endpoint would
otherwise need when the mobile client fires entry + closes in quick
succession.

Submission is sequential: entry first, then each close in input order. If
the entry fails, no close legs are submitted (no position to attach to).
Close failures don't roll back the entry — same partial-state semantics as
the homepage flow today.

200.description: Batch submitted (inspect per-leg `success` flags)

401.description: JWT missing, expired, or wallet mismatch

200.description: Cancel outcome. `success=false` with an `error` message means the order bot rejected the request (e.g. order already executed, on-chain cancel ineligible).

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked JWT, or JWT wallet doesn't match `wallet`

200.description: Collateral-edit outcome. `success=false` with an `error` message means the order bot rejected the edit (e.g. removing more collateral than available, leverage out of bounds).

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked JWT, or JWT wallet doesn't match `wallet`

200.description: Update outcome. `success=false` with an `error` message means the order bot rejected the update (e.g. order already filled or cancelled, native limit-order updates not supported).

401.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked JWT, or JWT wallet doesn't match `wallet`

200.description: JWT was added to the revocation list

parameters.name: underwriter
parameters.description:
Optional underwriter filter. Accepts `jupiter`, `flash_trade`,
`phoenix`, or `gmtrade`. Other values yield 400.

200.description: All open and recently-resolved orders for the wallet, across all 6 profile subaccounts. The `passthroughOrders` array carries every Imperial-routed resting order — Jupiter, Flash, Phoenix, and GMTrade alike — including TP/SL, limit, stop-limit, ratchet, DCA, and Phoenix native limits; the `jupiterOrders` array carries only Jupiter-native limit orders submitted through Jupiter's own (non-Imperial) limit-order program. Use this endpoint rather than `/passthrough/users/{wallet}/orders` when you want both classes in one call.
List of passthrough orders (open, executed, cancelled)

Idempotent sweep trigger. After closing positions, your profile may hold WSOL / WBTC / WETH residue (collateral returned in the market token rather than USDC). This endpoint asks the operator to swap those back to USDC and route the proceeds to the user wallet. Call after every Decrease that fully closes a non-USDC-collateralized position. Rate-limited per profile (~10s lock) — concurrent calls return `"queued"`.

400.description: Invalid wallet pubkey or out-of-range profile_index

200.description: Live Phoenix orderbook snapshots keyed by Phoenix-raw symbol (e.g. `GOLD`, not the Imperial-canonical `XAU`). The cache holds a persistent WS connection to `wss://perp-api.phoenix.trade/ws`; entries are at most 60s stale.
Per-symbol mid prices from the live Phoenix orderbook. Symbols are Phoenix-raw (e.g. `GOLD`, not `XAU`).

Phoenix CLOB markets, keyed by Phoenix's raw symbol (e.g. `GOLD` not the Imperial-canonical `XAU`). Includes max leverage from the smallest tier, taker/maker fees in millionths, and the orderbook PDA.

post.summary: Activates a wallet's Phoenix profile under Imperial's referral code.
post.description:
**Intentionally unauthenticated.** Imperial's Phoenix invite quota is
effectively unlimited, so we don't gate this on a wallet signature or JWT
— adding friction (sign-message prompt, JWT exchange) for a no-cost
activation hurts UX without protecting anything that matters. The
activation is keyed on the profile PDA derived from `wallet`; the
attacker-controlled wallet still has no on-chain authority over a
stranger's profile, so the worst a third party can do is pre-register a
wallet under our referral, which is exactly what we want anyway.

Do NOT add auth here without first checking with the team — see the
review thread on phoenix-native.

200.description: Activates the Phoenix trader account for `(wallet, profileIndex)` under Imperial's referral. **Optional for bots**: `/mobile/orders` already auto-activates Phoenix on first use and caches the result. Use this endpoint only when you want to pre-activate from a UI flow or warm the cache before sending a latency-sensitive first Phoenix order. Idempotent.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
Inclusive start (unix seconds). Defaults to `now - 90d` for `1h` and
the wallet's earliest action for `1d` (within the underwriter filter
when set).

parameters.name: underwriter
parameters.description:
Optional underwriter filter. Accepts `jupiter`, `flash_trade`,
`phoenix`, or `gmtrade`. Other values yield 400. When set, every
component (realized PnL, funding, taker fees, unrealized PnL) is
scoped to that venue.

200.description: Time-bucketed PnL history for the wallet, aggregated across every profile PDA. One row per `[since, until]` bucket (continuous — empty buckets carry forward the prior cumulative values). `unrealizedPnl` is populated only on the last bucket; earlier buckets serialize as null because historical mark-to-market is not supported.

400.description: Invalid wallet, resolution, range, or lookback exceeds the hourly cap (90 days).

get.summary:
Get open position lifecycles across every underwriter (Jupiter, Flash,
Phoenix, GMTrade), enriched with live PnL where available. Despite this
function's name, the response is multi-venue — the historical name predates
the broader underwriter set.

parameters.name: underwriter
parameters.description:
Optional underwriter filter. Accepts `jupiter`, `flash_trade`,
`phoenix`, or `gmtrade`. Other values yield 400.

200.description: Open positions for the wallet across all profiles and underwriters, with live PnL, leverage, entry price, liquidation price, accrued fees, and any attached TP/SL/ratchet/DCA close orders. Numeric fields are stringified to preserve precision.
Suggested Solana priority fee for trades against the passthrough program. Median over recent blocks, polled every 30s, capped at 500_000 micro-lamports.

parameters.name: notional
parameters.description: Trade notional in USD (collateral × leverage at entry).

parameters.name: holdHours
parameters.description:
Expected hold period in hours. Defaults to 24h. Drives the borrow term
and the Brownian first-passage probability used in the liquidation cost.

parameters.name: dailyVol
parameters.description:
Optional caller-supplied daily volatility (decimal, e.g. 0.04 = 4%/day).
Falls back to the asset-class table when absent.

parameters.name: stickyVenue
parameters.description:
Optional sticky-venue override. If provided, the router treats this venue
as already locked for the asset and returns it (clamped if leverage exceeds
the venue's cap). When `wallet` + `profileIndex` are present and `stickyVenue`
is omitted, the sticky venue is auto-resolved from open lifecycles.

parameters.name: subaccountDefaultVenue
parameters.description:
Optional per-subaccount default-venue override. Used when no sticky venue
applies and the asset is supported by the default venue at the requested
leverage.

parameters.name: wallet
parameters.description:
Optional wallet (base58). When present along with `profileIndex`, the
handler queries `position_lifecycles` for an open position on this asset
and uses that venue as the sticky.

parameters.name: excludedVenues
parameters.description:
Optional comma-separated list of venues the caller wants excluded
(e.g. `excludedVenues=phoenix,gmtrade`). When omitted, the handler
falls back to `profile_names.excluded_venues` via `wallet` +
`profile_index` if those are present. Sticky venues with open
positions still route there even if excluded — open positions take
precedence over exclusions.

200.description: Best venue for the trade plus a per-venue cost breakdown. `costBreakdown.expectedLiqCost = pLiq × LossGivenLiq(venue)`, where `pLiq` is the Brownian first-passage probability that the position hits its liquidation barrier within the requested `holdHours` window (default 24h): `pLiq = 2 × Φ(−d / σ_period)` with `d = 1/leverage − mmr` and `σ_period = dailyVol × √(holdHours / 24)`. Pass `holdHours` if your strategy holds positions for materially longer or shorter than a day.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Volume lookback window: `24h`, `7d`, or `30d`. Default: `24h`. OI is always a live snapshot.

200.description: Per-asset volume + open-interest breakdown. Symbols active in either source appear; missing fields zero-fill.
Current open-interest snapshot. Each row carries long/short/total notional plus distinct-trader and position counts.

parameters.name: grouping
parameters.description: Bucket size: `hour`, `day`, or `week`. Default: `hour` for 24h, `day` otherwise.

200.description: Historical open-interest time series, derived from the signed `tx2_size_usd_delta` event log (no snapshot table). `currentOiUsd` is the live truth — compare against the last row to spot indexer drift.
Protocol-level snapshot: 24h/7d/all-time volume, current open interest, 24h active traders and fee revenue, plus a per-venue breakdown.

parameters.name: grouping
parameters.description: Bucket size: `hour`, `day`, or `week`. Default: `hour` for 24h, `day` otherwise.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Restrict to one venue: `jupiter`, `flash`, `phoenix`, or `gmtrade`. Per-venue columns still split out all four (others zero-fill).

200.description: Time-series notional volume bucketed over the requested window. Volume counts opens/increases only — closes are excluded.
Service health snapshot — always returns 200, inspect fields for the actual state.

get.summary:
Get position lifecycles (open + closed) with their actions, paginated.
Multi-venue: returns lifecycles across every underwriter — the function
name is historical. Includes lazy background sync for Jupiter direct
positions on stale wallets.

parameters.name: underwriter
parameters.description:
Optional underwriter filter. Accepts `jupiter`, `flash_trade`,
`phoenix`, or `gmtrade`. Other values yield 400.

200.description: Position lifecycles for the wallet, newest first, paginated. Each lifecycle carries its full action history (entry, increases, decreases, close).

amount.description: Deposit amount in USDC native units (6-decimal — `1_000_000` = $1).

mode.description:
`"deposit"` to credit USDC directly from the wallet's USDC ATA into the
profile's USDC ATA, or `"withdraw"` to pull USDC out of the profile back
to the wallet's USDC ATA. Funding from native SOL is not supported —
wrap to WSOL on the client first.

profileIndex.description: Sub-account index (0..5) the deposit lands in.

wallet.description:
Owner wallet base58 pubkey. The returned tx is partially signed by the
order-bot operator (sponsoring rent + ATA creation) and must be signed
by this wallet before submission.

transaction.description:
Base64-encoded `VersionedTransaction`. Decode, sign with the wallet's
keypair, and submit via your usual RPC.

closeFee.description: Close fee weighted by `(1 − pLiq)` — expected amount paid on voluntary close.

fetchedAt.description:
Wall-clock time the most recent message landed, in milliseconds since
the unix epoch. Use this to gate routing on freshness (drop Phoenix
when `now − fetched_at > STALE_THRESHOLD_MS`).

GmTradeFundingRatesResponse.description:
Per-symbol funding/borrow/net rates for the bot use case. All fields are
percentages (0.04 = 0.04%) at hourly cadence, sourced from the GMTrade WS
`indexTokens` feed and refreshed every 5 minutes. Sign convention follows
GMTrade's: a positive `longFundingRatePerHourPercent` means longs pay shorts.

GmTradeLiquidityResponse.description:
Per-symbol pool headroom (USD) for the preferred (open, USDC-collateral
first) market variant. `null` on either side means the on-chain liquidity
poller hasn't seen that symbol yet, or the data couldn't be parsed.

profiles.description: One entry per Imperial subaccount (profile_index 0..5), in order.

orderPda.description: Base58 PDA of the order to cancel.

profileIndex.description: Sub-account index (0..5) the order belongs to.

wallet.description: Base58 wallet pubkey. Must match the JWT's wallet claim.

collateralAmount.description: Amount of collateral to add or remove, in the collateral mint's native units.

marketMint.description: Base58 SPL mint of the market whose position is being adjusted.

underwriter.description:
0 = Jupiter, 1 = Flash Trade, 2 = Phoenix, 3 = GMTrade. (4 reserved
for Pacifica.) Wire format mirrors `passthrough_client::Underwriter`.

wallet.description: Base58 wallet pubkey. Must match the JWT's wallet claim.

message.description: Plain message the wallet signed. Must be `imperial:mobile-connect:{wallet}:{nonce}`.

signature.description: Base58-encoded signature over `message` by `wallet`'s private key.

code.description: One-time code to exchange for a JWT via `/mobile/exchange` within 5 minutes.

closeOrders.description:
Close-side legs (TP, SL, ratchet, DCA-close, ...) attached to the entry.
Each must have `action == 1` and match the entry's
`(wallet, marketMint, side, underwriter, profileIndex)` tuple.
One result per close leg, in input order. Empty if the entry failed.

entry.description: Result of the entry submission. `success = false` skips the close legs.

collateralAmount.description: Collateral amount in the collateral mint's native units.

extraData.description:
Per-order-type parameters (e.g. `{ "worstPrice": ..., "ratchetSize": ... }`
for ratchet orders; `{ "dcaNumLegs": ..., "dcaIntervalSeconds": ... }` for DCA).

fundingStatus.description:
Funding mode: 0 = funded at creation (collateral debited from the profile immediately),
1 = pending (the order is created without collateral and the order-bot funder tops it up
asynchronously). Most integrators want 0.

marketMint.description:
Base58 SPL mint of the market (e.g. SOL mint for SOL-PERP). Either this
or `symbol` must be set; if both are provided, `marketMint` wins.
Use `symbol` instead unless you already have a venue-specific mint and
want to skip the server-side lookup.

marketPrice.description:
Client-observed market price (oracle scale, 1e9) at the moment the order was placed.
Forwarded to the on-chain market-order instruction so the program can enforce slippage
against an agreed-upon reference price. Ignored for resting orders.

parentOrderPda.description:
Optional Order PDA of the parent limit order this close order is
attached to. Set this when adding a TP/SL/Trailing to a still-resting
limit so the UI groups it under that limit (instead of relying on the
`(market, side, profile)` heuristic, which mis-groups close orders
across unrelated parents on the same key). NULL for standalone close
orders (i.e. attached to an open position) and for entry orders.

phoenixNative.description:
Opt into Phoenix's native CLOB for this order instead of the
keeper-managed IOC fallback. Only meaningful when `underwriter = 2`
(Phoenix) AND `orderType` is `Limit` (1) with `action = Increase` (0),
or `PrivateTpSl` (5). Ignored for any other shape.

Default behavior (unset / `false`): the API rewrites Phoenix Limit /
PrivateTpSl to StopLimit so the order routes through the keeper path.
That dodges a class of indexer + orderbook-watcher bugs that have
stranded user collateral in the past (cancel-cleanup races, slot
classification edges).

Set `true` only if you understand those risks and need genuine resting
CLOB orders — e.g. maker-fee arbitrage bots. Orders placed with
`phoenixNative = true` will not be rewritten and will rest natively on
the Phoenix orderbook.

priority.description:
Funding-queue ranking for resting orders. Lower value = higher priority (0 is highest).
Used by the order-bot funder to decide which `funding_status=1` orders to top up first
when the profile has constrained balance. Has no effect on Solana priority fees.

profileIndex.description: Sub-account index (0..5). Each profile is an isolated margin account.

sizeUsd.description: Position size in USD, 6-decimal fixed point (1_000_000 = $1).

symbol.description:
Canonical Imperial symbol (e.g. `"SOL"`, `"XAU"`). Resolved server-side
to the right `marketMint` for the requested `underwriter` — this is the
recommended addressing path: the same symbol you read from
`/funding-rates` works directly here, including Phoenix synthetics
(XAU, SPY, EUR, GBP, …) that have no SPL mint of their own.

triggerCondition.description: 0 = Above, 1 = Below. Direction of trigger condition relative to price.

triggerPrice.description: Trigger price in oracle scale (1e9). Required for non-market orders.

underwriter.description:
0 = Jupiter, 1 = Flash Trade, 2 = Phoenix, 3 = GMTrade. (4 reserved
for Pacifica.) Wire format mirrors `passthrough_client::Underwriter`.

wallet.description: Base58 wallet pubkey. Must match the JWT's wallet claim.

expiresAt.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the JWT expires.

jwt.description: JWT. Pass as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` on trading endpoints.

error.description: Humanized error message when `success` is false.

orderPda.description:
Base58 Order PDA for **resting orders only** (limit, stop-limit, ratchet,
DCA, Phoenix native, etc.) — `None` for market orders, which open a
position rather than rest. Use this for downstream `/mobile/orders/cancel`
or `/mobile/orders/update` calls without polling the indexer to discover
the PDA. Deterministic — derived from the profile PDA + the
`user_account.order_counter` snapshot at placement.

signature.description: Solana transaction signature, if one was produced.

success.description: True if the order bot accepted the request.

closeBps.description: For decrease orders: new close percentage in bps (0..10000). Omit to leave unchanged.

orderPda.description: Base58 PDA of the order to update.

priority.description: New Solana priority fee tier. Omit to leave unchanged.

oneOf.description: Per-type parameter update for advanced orders (ratchet / land-mine).

sizeUsd.description: New size in USD. Omit to leave unchanged.

slippageBps.description: New slippage tolerance (bps). Omit to leave unchanged.

triggerPrice.description: New trigger price (oracle scale). Omit to leave unchanged.

wallet.description: Base58 wallet pubkey. Must match the JWT's wallet claim.

currentOiUsd.description:
Live current open interest (sum of `status='open'` lifecycles). Compare
against `rows`' last entry — divergence indicates indexer drift, not
arithmetic error in this endpoint.

creationSlot.description:
Solana slot at which the placement tx landed. The FE compares this
against the slot of its locally-cached Phoenix orderbook snapshot
before classifying a missing slot as "removed from book" — a
snapshot from a slot before this one couldn't have observed the
placement yet.

parentOrderPda.description:
Order PDA of the parent limit, when this is a close order (TP/SL/
Trailing) attached to a still-resting limit. The FE uses this
instead of the legacy `(market, side, profile)` heuristic to group
close orders under their parent. `None` for entry orders and for
close orders attached to an open position.

venueOrderState.description:
Per-venue resting state — Phoenix native carries (price_in_ticks,
order_sequence_number, initial_base_lots, remaining_base_lots,
trader_account_pda) so the FE can subscribe to the orderbook and
render live partial-fill progress. None for venues with no
resting state.

creationSlot.description:
Solana slot at which the placement tx landed. The FE compares this
against the slot of its locally-cached Phoenix orderbook snapshot
before deciding the order is "gone from book" — a snapshot from a
slot before this one couldn't have observed the placement, so we
must NOT classify a missing slot in that snapshot as a removal.

parentOrderPda.description:
Order PDA of the parent limit, when this is a close order (TP/SL/
Trailing) attached to a still-resting limit. The FE uses this
instead of the legacy `(market, side, profile)` heuristic to group
close orders under their parent — explicit links don't mis-attach
across markets/profiles. `None` for entry orders and for close
orders attached to an open position.

venueOrderState.description:
Per-venue runtime state of the resting order. For Phoenix-native
limits this carries `(price_in_ticks, order_sequence_number,
remaining_base_lots, trader_account_pda, …)` — the FE uses these
to scan the Phoenix orderbook account directly and render live
fill progress (X of Y filled). `None` for venues with no resting
state (Jupiter / Flash IOC fills, Phoenix native that hasn't
landed on the book yet).

PhoenixMarkPrice.description:
Mid-price snapshot per Phoenix-raw symbol. The mid is `(best_bid + best_ask) / 2`
from the live orderbook WS feed, computed in `handle_text` and stored on the
snapshot. Empty until the WS connection has received its first orderbook
message; stale entries (no message in 60s) are filtered by `get_fresh`.

fetchedAt.description: Wall-clock time the most recent message landed, in milliseconds since epoch.

baseLotsDecimals.description:
Signed because Phoenix publishes negative values for some markets
(e.g. PUMP = -2). Mirrors `PhoenixMarketInfo.base_lots_decimals`.

maxLeverage.description: Max leverage from the first (smallest position) tier

maxSizeBaseLots.description: Max position size in base lots from the first tier

cumulativeFundingPayment.description:
Cumulative funding/interest paid (Phoenix funding + Jupiter borrow
rate + GMTrade funding). Signed: positive = paid out by trader.

cumulativePnl.description: Realized PnL accumulated up to and including this bucket. USD float.

cumulativeTakerFee.description:
Cumulative taker / order / venue execution fees across underwriters.
Excludes Imperial's platform fee and DEX swap fees.

unrealizedPnl.description:
Mark-to-market PnL of currently open positions at the time of the
request. **Only present on the last bucket** — historical
mark-to-market is not supported because we do not persist historical
mark prices. All earlier buckets serialize this field as `null`.

priority_fee.description:
Median Solana priority fee (micro-lamports per CU) observed across
recent blocks that touched the passthrough program. Polled every 30s
and clamped to a 500_000 ceiling so a stuck whale doesn't make the
suggestion useless. Field is `priority_fee` (snake_case) — this
endpoint predates the camelCase convention and stays as-is for
existing integrators.

usdc.description:
USDC balance held in the profile's USDC ATA, in raw native units
(6-decimal — divide by `1_000_000` for whole USDC). `0` when the ATA
hasn't been created yet.

RateSource.description:
Provenance tag for a per-venue rate entry. Helps a bot author distinguish
live data from fallbacks without parsing free-form text.

profileIndex.description:
Sub-account index (0..5) being activated. Each profile activates
independently — bots farming across multiple subaccounts must register
each one before placing Phoenix orders from it.

wallet.description:
Owner wallet base58 pubkey. The activation is keyed on the profile PDA
derived from `wallet` + `profileIndex`.

marketsVersion.description:
Stable hash over the cross-venue market universe at request time. Two
responses with the same `marketsVersion` were computed against the same
market list — partners poll this to detect drift in the supported set
without re-snapshotting every venue. Set by the handler post-`compute_route`.

oneOf.description:
Pre-sweep snapshot of the profile's tokenized residue. Present whenever
the response had something to act on — useful for the bot to confirm
post-close what was reclaimed. Token amounts are in their respective
native scales (SOL: lamports / 1e9, BTC: 1e8, ETH: 1e8).

status.description:
`"clean"` — nothing to sweep (no token-form residue on the profile).
`"queued"` — a sweep was already triggered recently; this call is a
no-op until the lock expires (~10s).
`"swept"` — the operator submitted sweep instructions to bring residual
WSOL/WBTC/WETH back to the user wallet as USDC.

TpSlOrderResponse.description: Unified TP/SL order response - represents orders from both Jupiter native and Imperial passthrough

closeBps.description:
Decrease-only close percentage in basis points (10000 = 100%). The
on-chain Order carries this so the program can apply it to live
position size at execution; surfacing it here lets the FE render the
user's chosen percentage without falling back to the
`orderSizeUsd / positionSizeUsd` ratio (which drifts above 100% when
the parent limit fills slightly under-size). `None` for legacy rows
created before the indexer started splatting `close_bps` into
`passthrough_orders.order_data`.

borrowHourlyPct.description:
Hourly borrow/funding cost as a percent of notional. JLP-style venues charge
borrow on utilized liquidity; CLOBs run zero-sum funding and pass ~0.

oneOf.description:
Side-specific override for `borrow_hourly_pct`. Required for GMTrade where long
and short pay different combined funding+borrow rates. Negative entries mean the
venue pays the trader on that side; cost computation clamps to 0.

mmr.description:
Maintenance margin ratio (fraction of notional). Liquidation triggers when
residual collateral falls below `mmr × notional`.

longBorrowRatePerHourPercent.description:
Hourly borrow rate in percent. Always non-negative — the side holding
the position pays this regardless of funding direction. Set even when
only one side borrows (Jupiter longs pay borrow on the long custody;
shorts on the short/USDC custody — both sides populated).

longFundingRatePerHourPercent.description:
Hourly funding rate in percent (`0.04` = 0.04%). Sign convention is
uniform across every venue this API serves: **positive long rate ⇒
longs pay shorts** (i.e. shorts receive); negative ⇒ shorts pay longs.
GMTrade rates are passed through raw from the venue WS feed (which
already follows that convention); Phoenix exposes a single signed
rate so the short-side value is the negation of the long-side. For
Jupiter and Flash this field is `None` — those venues charge borrow
fees instead, see `*_borrow_rate_*`.

fetchedAtUnixMs.description:
Wall-clock time the underlying snapshot was last refreshed, in
milliseconds since epoch. For Phoenix this is the WS message timestamp;
for Flash it's the CustomOracle's `publish_time` (Pyth-pushed but
trails Hermes by however long Flash's keeper takes).

bearer_jwt.description: JWT issued via `/mobile/connect` + `/mobile/exchange`. The wallet claim in the JWT must match the `wallet` field in each trading request body.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Connect a wallet, issue a JWT, revoke a JWT

tags.name: trading
tags.description: Submit, cancel, and update orders. JWT required.

tags.name: reads
tags.description: Public read endpoints. No auth required.
